title: Aviator Demo — Simple Aviator-Style Crash Game Demo (Frontend)

requirements.summary: Implement a single-page Aviator-style crash game round loop with Start/Cash Out controls, multiplier tick, random crash point, and result text.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI shows a title “Simple Aviator Game (Demo)” and a multiplier display initialized at 1.00x.
Clicking Start begins the round; multiplier increases by 0.05 every 100ms and updates the display formatted to 2 decimals with an “x” suffix.
The game chooses a random crash point between 1.00x and 6.00x (inclusive range as implemented by 1 + Math.random()*5) and ends the round when multiplier reaches/exceeds it.
On crash, the UI displays exactly “Crashed at {crashPoint}x” (English text) with the crashPoint shown to 2 decimals.
Clicking Cash Out while running stops the round and displays exactly “Cashed out at {multiplier}x” with multiplier shown to 2 decimals.
Start cannot start a new round while a round is already running.

file_operations.description: Create the main single-page game component implementing the round state machine (idle/running/crashed/cashed-out), 100ms timer tick (+0.05), random crash point (1 + Math.random()*5), and Start/Cash Out control logic. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Button/Card) for controls/layout to satisfy the existing UI components constraint; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire the AviatorCrashDemo component into the app as the only rendered page (single-page demo). Use shadcn-ui layout primitives if available to keep structure consistent; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Apply a cohesive dark “night sky cockpit” visual theme using Tailwind with a non-blue/non-purple primary accent while keeping the UI minimal.
acceptanceCriteria: The app has a consistent dark theme with a clear accent color that is not primarily blue or purple.
Typography, spacing, and button styles look intentional and consistent across the page.
The multiplier display is visually emphasized (larger size, accent color) similar in prominence to the provided HTML demo.

file_operations.description: Add Tailwind base/components/utilities and define theme CSS variables for a dark cockpit palette with a mint/green accent (avoid blue/purple as primary). Ensure consistent typography, spacing, and button focus/hover states using Tailwind-friendly tokens.

Apply Tailwind classes for the dark cockpit layout: centered minimal stack (title, multiplier, controls, result), emphasized multiplier styling with mint accent, and consistent button styling (prefer shadcn-ui Button variants if available); verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add required generated static image assets under frontend/public/assets/generated and render at least one in the UI without any backend image routing.
acceptanceCriteria: All required generated images exist under frontend/public/assets/generated.
The UI renders at least one of the generated images (e.g., in the header or as a subtle background element) without any backend image routing.

file_operations.description: Add generated image asset: Minimal airplane silhouette icon, flat vector style, mint accent on dark background.
Add generated image asset: Wide night-sky gradient background with subtle stars and a faint horizon glow.

Render at least one generated image in the UI (e.g., show the airplane icon in the header and/or apply the sky background as a subtle page background) by referencing the static files at frontend/public/assets/generated/aviator-plane-icon.dim_256x256.png and/or frontend/public/assets/generated/aviator-sky-bg.dim_1920x1080.png.

requirements.summary: Add English documentation with clear local and Internet Computer run/deploy instructions including where to find the local URL and deployed canister URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation includes local run instructions and explicitly states the local URL to open the app.
Documentation includes Internet Computer deployment steps (dfx deploy) and explains how to obtain/open the deployed frontend canister URL.

file_operations.description: Write English project documentation covering: local setup/run steps, the local URL to open (e.g., the dev server URL), Internet Computer deployment steps (including running dfx deploy), and how to obtain/open the deployed frontend canister URL after deployment.
